title: Gliricidia ehrenbergii
taxonomy: Gliricidia ehrenbergii is classified as a species of tree in the family Fabaceae, which is a part of the order Fabales. It is further categorized as a member of the genus Gliricidia, which contains several species of trees and shrubs native to the tropical regions of the Americas and Africa. The exact classification of Gliricidia ehrenbergii can be broken down into its binomial nomenclature, which is Gliricidia ehrenbergii. The species is also sometimes referred to as a synonym for Gliricidia sepium or Gliricidia maculata. However, the exact classification may vary depending on the source and region.
uses: Gliricidia ehrenbergii, a plant native to tropical regions, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and bark are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin infections. The plant's extracts are also believed to have antimalarial and antihelmintic properties, making it a potential natural remedy for malaria and intestinal parasites. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Gliricidia ehrenbergii for medicinal purposes. Its potential uses in traditional medicine highlight the importance of preserving and studying the plant's unique properties.

Its leaves are rich in antioxidants and have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever and rheumatism.
